zh.Rottingdean, Brighton, Sussex, England: 英國英格蘭素可洛廷格迪恩
zh.lifeSummary: Elizabeth MNU Hogg於1492年出生在英國英格蘭素可洛廷格迪恩。她於1518年與Richard George Beard在英國英格蘭素可結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。她去世於1526年1月1日在英國英格蘭素可享年34歲。

zh-hans.Rottingdean, Brighton, Sussex, England: 英国英格兰素可洛廷格迪恩
zh-hans.lifeSummary: Elizabeth MNU Hogg于1492年出生在英国英格兰素可洛廷格迪恩。她于1518年与Richard George Beard在英国英格兰素可结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。她去世于1526年1月1日在英国英格兰素可享年34岁。

nameMeanings.name: Hogg
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (northern England and Scotland): nickname for a swineherd or shepherd, from Middle English hog(ge) (Old English hogg) denoting either a pig, especially a castrated one, or a young sheep before its first shearing (the latter sense is most likely in northern England).
German (Högg): topographic name, a variant of Heck 1, found chiefly in Bavaria.

variants.name: Heck
meanings: South German: topographic name from Middle High German hecke, hegge ‘hedge’. This surname is common in southern Germany and the Rhineland, and is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine).
Dutch: topographic name from Middle Dutch heck ‘fence, gate’. Compare Van Hecke .
English: topographic name for someone who lived by a gate or ‘hatch’ (especially one leading into a forest), northern Middle English heck (Old English hæcce). Compare Hatch .

variants.name: Hoggard
meanings: English (East Yorkshire): occupational name from Middle English hog(ge)+ herd(e), hird indicating either a ‘swineherd’ or, in northern England, more likely ‘one who looks after the hogs or young, unshorn sheep’ (see Hogg ).

variants.name: Hoag
meanings: Scottish and English: variant of   Hogg  .

Americanized form of Swedish Hög: ornamental or topographic name from hög ‘hill, mound’, or a habitational name from a place called Hög or containing the word hög ‘high’, e.g. Högfors ‘high waterfall’.

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

nameMeanings.name: MNU
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Cheng, Chia, Chih Chung, Ching, Kai Chung, Kuen, Mei, Wah, Xue, Yiwen, Yung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 牟, meaning ‘mooing or bellowing’ in ancient Chinese: from Mou (牟), the name of an ancient state (located in Laiwu in Shandong province) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ).
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surname 毛, based on its Cantonese pronunciation, see   Mao   1.

variants.name: Mao
meanings: ‘Honest’; also short for any name beginning with Nao-.

en.lifeSummary: Elizabeth MNU Hogg was born in 1492, in Rottingdean, Sussex, England, United Kingdom. She married Richard George Beard in 1518, in Sussex, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. She died on 1 January 1526, in Sussex, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 34.

fr.lifeSummary: Elizabeth MNU Hogg est née en 1492, à Rottingdean, Sussex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Elle a épousé Richard George Beard en 1518, à Sussex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Elle est décédée le 1 janvier 1526, à Sussex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 34 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Elizabeth MNU Hogg wurde 1492 in Rottingdean, Sussex, England, United Kingdom geboren. Sie hat 1518 in Sussex, England, United Kingdom Richard George Beard geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter. Sie starb am 1. Januar 1526 im Alter von 34 Jahren in Sussex, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Elizabeth MNU Hogg è nata il nel 1492 nel Rottingdean, Sussex, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Lei sposato Richard George Beard nel 1518, nel Sussex, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia. Morì il 1 gennaio 1526, nel Sussex, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 34 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth MNU Hogg(은)는 1492년 영국 잉글랜드 수세스 로틴그디에서 태어났다. 그녀는 1518년 영국 잉글랜드 수세스에서 Richard George Beard(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1526년 1월 1일 영국 잉글랜드 수세스에서 34세의 나이로 사망했다.
